title: blog-writer diagram DSL
description: High-level figure description that layout-scene.mjs expands into a positioned Excalidraw scene. The five built-in types cover the figure shapes the existing posts use; complex one-offs drop down to type:'raw'.

type.description: Selects the layout engine. pipeline=horizontal flow; stack=nested layers; before-after=two columns; matrix=row×col grid; tree=hierarchy; timeline=horizontal events; grid=free placement on N×M cells with optional rowspan/colspan and edges (this covers most ex-raw figures); graph=deterministic layered DAG with barycenter crossing-reduction (must branch/merge — a one-node-per-layer flow is a pipeline, not a graph); raw=hand-authored elements (last resort).

title.description: Short, sentence-case figure title (rendered at 32-pt Virgil at the top).

caption.description: One sentence describing what the reader should take away. NOT a label restatement. Rendered at 28-pt Virgil under the title.

claim.description: ≥ 8-word sentence stating the single provable claim the figure makes. Validator-checked.

anchor.description: Markdown heading id or section anchor the node illustrates. Used by Phase E faithfulness gate.

cells.description: matrix only — rows × cols of {label, kind}

raw.description: Escape hatch for type:'raw' — passes elements straight through to author-scene.mjs.

positions.description: Optional id→{x,y,w,h} so overlay callouts can target raw nodes.

events.description: timeline only — left-to-right ordered events with {id, label, kind, date?}.

date.description: Optional date / version tag rendered above the label.

gridRows.description: grid only — explicit row count; defaults to max(node.row+rowspan).

gridCols.description: grid only — explicit col count; defaults to max(node.col+colspan).

overlay.description: Works on ANY type — list of callouts: small Cascadia text + dashed arrow pointing at a node from a side. Use this instead of dropping to raw when an existing layout type just needs an annotation or two.

target.description: id of the node the callout points at
